title: How NCRP/1930 actually works
body: Report immediately — the "golden hour" is real, not just advice. India's Citizen Financial Cyber Fraud Reporting System (I4C, operational since 2021) relays your report in near real-time to banks and payment apps so they can freeze the fraudulent transfer before it's moved further ("layered") and withdrawn. As of the government's most recently published figures, this system has helped save over ₹8,189 crore across more than 23.6 lakh complaints. Call 1930 (free, 24/7, all major Indian languages) or file at cybercrime.gov.in — filing costs nothing, and sensitive categories can be reported anonymously.
what_to_do: Call 1930 immediately, or file a complaint at cybercrime.gov.in. Keep your bank transaction details, UPI ID, and screenshots ready — acting fast materially improves the chance of freezing the transfer before funds are moved further.

source.name: I4C / PIB press release on the Citizen Financial Cyber Fraud Reporting and Management System (CFCFRMS)

title: RBI's customer-liability rules for unauthorized bank transactions
body: Under RBI's 2017 circular (RBI/2017-18/15, "Customer Protection – Limiting Liability of Customers in Unauthorised Electronic Banking Transactions"), you have zero liability if the fraud was the bank's fault or a third-party breach AND you reported it within 3 working days of the bank notifying you. Reporting later means liability is limited on a sliding scale, not automatically total. Once you report, the bank must provisionally credit the disputed amount to your account within 10 working days while it investigates (investigation window up to 90 days).
what_to_do: Report any unauthorized transaction to your bank in writing (call + email/app) the moment you notice it — the 3-working-day clock runs from when the bank notifies you, not from when you personally discover it.

source.name: RBI Circular RBI/2017-18/15 — Customer Protection: Limiting Liability of Customers in Unauthorised Electronic Banking Transactions

title: DPDP Act 2023 basics — your data rights
body: Under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (No. 22 of 2023), you have a right to know what personal data is collected about you and why, a right to withdraw consent at any time, and a right to file a grievance — escalating to the Data Protection Board of India if a data fiduciary doesn't resolve it. In scam context: a caller demanding your Aadhaar, OTP, or bank details without a lawful, consented purpose isn't just risking fraud — it's asking you to bypass a right the law already gives you to control that data.
what_to_do: Never share OTP, Aadhaar, or bank credentials with an unverified caller — no lawful data request requires reading a one-time code aloud over a call.

source.name: Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, No. 22 of 2023

title: Your rights when reporting cybercrime
body: Filing an NCRP complaint is always free — there is no fee at any step. You receive a complaint/acknowledgment number to track status. Certain sensitive categories (e.g. crimes against women/children) can be reported anonymously. This process applies regardless of the amount involved — there is no threshold below which a complaint is "too small to report."
what_to_do: File the complaint even for a small amount or a near-miss (no money lost yet) — it costs nothing and helps flag the number/account for others.

source.name: National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal (NCRP), cybercrime.gov.in

title: What I4C and Chakshu actually do (and don't)
body: I4C (Indian Cyber Crime Coordination Centre, under the Ministry of Home Affairs) coordinates India's national cybercrime response and issues public advisories — it's the body behind 1930 and the NCRP portal. Chakshu, its citizen-facing reporting facility (part of the Sanchar Saathi platform), is a one-way intake: you can report a suspected fraud communication (call/SMS/WhatsApp) to it, but it has no public lookup or reputation API — it cannot tell you in advance whether a given number is a scammer.
what_to_do: Use Chakshu/Sanchar Saathi to report a suspicious number after the fact; don't expect it to verify a number for you before you answer or call back.

source.name: I4C public advisories (Ministry of Home Affairs)
